home.taglineSub: Document, share and track the architectural choices that matter.
home.feature1Desc: Record the context, options considered and consequences of each technical decision.
home.feature2Desc: Group decisions by project and keep everything tidy and accessible.
home.feature3Desc: Collaborate with your team and keep everyone aligned on the choices made.

hero.eyebrow: Technical decision log for modern teams
hero.headline: Keep your decisions in control. Enforced in CI.
hero.subheadline: Decern is where you record your team's technical decisions and link them to pull requests. A small gate runs in your CI pipeline and checks that every change, including AI-generated code, respects what you actually decided.
hero.bullet1: Stop code that breaks your architecture from reaching main
hero.bullet2: One place for decisions; automatic checks on every PR
hero.bullet3: No more hunting through docs, Slack, or meeting notes

problem.title: Decisions everywhere, control nowhere
problem.intro: Where do your team's technical decisions live? In Confluence, Notion, a markdown file, Slack, or last week's meeting notes. When a new pull request lands, nobody checks if it still matches what you decided months ago. And now that AI writes more and more code, the gap gets worse: the AI doesn't know your rules. It can skip layers, ignore security choices, or bring back patterns you wanted to drop.
problem.example1: Ever seen a PR that added a new layer and bypassed the pattern you'd agreed on?
problem.example2: Or a refactor that was supposed to remove an old approach, and the next PR brought it back because nobody was checking?
problem.example3: With AI-generated code it's even riskier: lots of output, zero memory of your decisions.

solution.title: Four simple steps
solution.step1Body: Write your technical decision in Decern (context, options, what you chose, and why). You can draft with AI, but a human reviews and approves. Everything in one place.
solution.step2Body: In the PR description, point to that decision. The PR now "carries" the decision and its tags (e.g. payments, security). Your pipeline knows which rules apply.
solution.step3Body: A small tool (decern-gate) runs in your CI (e.g. npx decern-gate). It reads the PR diff and the linked decision, and checks that nothing in the change breaks your rules: architecture, dependencies, security, or internal policies.
solution.step4Title: Block or warn when something's off
solution.step4Body: If something doesn't match, you get a comment on the PR and optionally a failed job. So neither the AI nor anyone else can merge code that goes against what the team agreed.

ai.title: Why this matters with AI
ai.intro: A few years ago the hard part was writing code. Now the hard part is making sure what gets written is what you want. AI gets from idea to code fast, but it doesn't know your architecture. Teams risk losing control not only over what was decided, but over what actually ships.
ai.point1: Decern turns your team's decisions into checks that run in CI.
ai.point2: It's the filter between "what the AI suggested" and "what goes to production."

features.title: Built for clarity and control
features.f1Desc: One place for every decision: context, options, outcome, and consequences. No more scattered docs or threads.
features.f2Desc: Attach decisions to pull requests. Your pipeline knows which rules apply to each change.
features.f3Desc: Choose whether violations fail the job or only get reported. On Free, we report without blocking.
features.f4Desc: Organize decisions by topic and enforce the right rules for each PR.
features.f5Desc: Over time you see where and when decisions were violated, so you can fix drift before it becomes a mess.
features.f6Title: AI and humans, in the right order
features.f6Desc: Your decisions set the rules. AI can suggest code, but it has to pass the same checks as everyone else.
features.f7Desc: Workspaces, projects, and roles so the right people own and approve decisions.
features.f8Desc: Paste meeting notes or a rough description and let AI draft a structured decision. You review, refine and approve. The thinking stays human.

useCases.title: Use cases
useCases.uc1Title: Teams that use AI every day
useCases.uc1Desc: You want to trust generated code because it goes through the same decisions and checks as the rest of your codebase.
useCases.uc2Desc: Make sure new PRs don't bring back patterns you're trying to remove. The gate keeps everyone on the same direction.
useCases.uc3Desc: Show what was decided and how it was enforced in CI: clear evidence for anyone who needs it.
useCases.uc4Desc: New teammates see the real rules in action on every PR. No need to dig through old wikis.

trust.title: Built for people who care about control
trust.claim1: For tech leads and architects who want more control and less paperwork.
trust.claim2: The only tool that combines a decision log, PR linking, and a CI gate in one place.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.q1: How is this different from Confluence, Notion, or ADRs in Markdown?
faq.a1: Those tools store documents but don't enforce them. Decern links decisions to PRs and runs checks in CI. If a PR breaks a decision, the pipeline can block or flag it. You also get a structured log (status, tags, links to PRs) you can search and report on.
faq.q2: How is this different from code review or static analysis?
faq.a2: Review and linters don't know your architectural decisions. Decern encodes those decisions and checks each PR against them. It adds a simple question: "Does this respect what we decided?"
faq.q3: Does the AI write the decisions for us?
faq.a3: You can use AI to draft a decision (e.g. from meeting notes), but a human reviews and approves. Decern's job is to enforce what humans decided, not to replace decision-making.
faq.q4: Won't this block our pipeline too often?
faq.a4: You choose. The gate can fail the job (strict) or only report issues (observation mode). On the Free plan we only report, so you can get used to it without blocking releases.
faq.q5: Do we need to change our GitHub or GitLab workflow?
faq.a5: Almost no change. You add a link to the decision in the PR description and one step in CI (e.g. npx decern-gate). Your process stays the same.

cta.title: Gate your decisions. Block code that breaks them before production.
cta.subline: One decision log. Linked to PRs. Checked in CI.
cta.ctaPrimary: Try Decern free with your team
